item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In the part where it says,

Pores sporadically contracted and expanded for seven hours before the dissolution of the tooth-like structures, during which time the subject's body returned to normal.

Is it supposed to be "after which the subject's body returned to normal," or is the churning teeth a slow process of renormalizing?

Other than that, fifthists and body horror I dont 100% understand the meaning of but wholeheartedly enjoy nonetheless. Here have a +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Me, a year after reading this for the first time, on page 153 of Neuromancer: "Ohhhhhhhhhhhhhhhh".

Good title, taff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You put a lot of work into that transcript, and it paid off. +1.

item.title: I don't get it
item.content:encoded: I've read it 3 times. I've read all the links and connection, even boxes, but I seriously can't see what this is. Am I plain dumb or I just can't read under the cover?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is disgusting! Yay! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is one of the most horrifying things I've read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel itching after read this…take your +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I failed to see that the distribution was apparently continuous. I was under the belief that the only way for the public to attain any of the product was to somehow know about the commercial aired on March, 25th 2014; my apologies. Previous statement recanted.

Congrats on 100 upvotes.

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded: Welcome to the cool kids club.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's Keter because a clearly anomalous product is making its way into the hands of civilians with no apparent source or clear way of stopping it from continuing to happen in future.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
An active nuclear warhead that's just sitting waiting for someone to detonate it would be Safe - if you lock it in a box, you're done. It's still dangerous, but it's not breaking out and it doesn't do anything until someone messes with it.

A nuclear warhead that has had its codes put in and is counting down to detonation would probably be Keter, though - it would require some kind of really difficult procedure to stop the explosion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's keter because it is Really hard to control. It appears semi-randomly, and en mass. Remember, The ratings are based on difficultly of containment, not danger.
A non-activated nuclear warhead would be safe, and a bee would be elucid, because it has to be kept alive.
An activated warhead would be keter, waiting to boom at any moment.
The flowers that feeds the bee would be thaumiel.
And for that newfangled Apollyon class that keeps appearing, it would be an activated warhead that could blow up the universe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Bringing up the Keter point everyone else has, the SCP is the soap pumps for the treatment of whatever this outbreak is (wasp acne), right? If that's so, then I have to agree and say this should be Safe. It sounds like The Foundation has a pretty okay grasp on where these things are and how many are getting out.

Everything good has been said. Good body horror, nice and squicky.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why is it Keter? I could understand Euclid…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is pretty much how I imagine that particular run-in going down.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

Great featured skip! I'm not overly familiar with various GOIs (like the Fifthists) but this works even without it.

+ Show Headcanon
- Hide Headcanon

While such labels usually appear similar to the labels of other contemporaneous Neutrogena products, Neutrogena has never manufactured a product named "Wasp Cleanser". When questioned, Neutrogena representatives stated that they had no intention to produce such a product any time in the foreseeable future.

So some Foundation Agent called customer service, and asked if they are ever planning on releasing a "Wasp Cleanser." When this was with laughter and derision, the Foundation Agent decided to kidnap some Executive Researcher from his home, and interrogate him in a dimly lit warehouse.

A: ARE YOU WORKING ON WASP CLEANSER? YES OR NO??!!
E: Please don't kill me! We'll make you Wasp Cleanser if you want it! I've got a whole case in my car, just let me go, and I'll show you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll upvote, but this probably shouldn't be Keter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay, might be just me but I'm not entirely sure what makes this skip a Keter. The effects didn't seem to be wholly fatal or immediately dangerous to a significant portion of the population, at least the ones described.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm thinking this is a possible future where the wasps from 2850 become virulent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I actually didn't know that. Makes sense in hindsight, I s'pose.

In actual fact, AbsentmindedNihilist suggested "wasps which were WASPs" as an SCP idea in chat one day. I thought of the appropriate skincare products such WASP wasps would need to get the wasps out of their pores and this was born.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Was this inspired by seeing that the website for Neutrogena has in it's title "Skin Care Products"?
I don't like who I am when I see a wasp.

item.title: Re: Anglo-Saxon Protestants Not Included
item.content:encoded:
I'm obviously massively in favour of cross-promotion of my work (!) - the link to Exit Interview has led to as many upvotes on that tale yesterday as in the preceding two months. Thanks very much for that!

You are of course more than welcome to link to Boxes. That said, I won't be headcanoning it - I quite like that tale existing in a liminal space where the role of the Foundation, and even the existence of the anomalous, is not certain.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Neutrogena does not endorse the use of its Waspaway™ products for purposes not related to the elimination of wasp-related problems. Should the consumer use the Neutrogena Wasp Cleanser for purposes not limited to but including:

  • the extermination of apian creatures
  • assassination of consistently undead presidents
  • clearing out ant infestations

they thereby declare their right to return the product null and void as per the consumer by-laws of the Overseer's Greater Britamerican Conglomeration.

– transcript of a label found attached to SCP-2858-39, the first SCP-2858 instance recovered post Addendum SCP-2858-1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*Looks at SCP-2401.*
I'll take ten of everything.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good idea. A little note added.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, this certainly is far better than it has any right to be. Was not expecting Fifthists to show up! (And the whole thing's a smokescreen? What the fuuuuck?)

several tooth-like structures began developing

WHAT THE FUUUUUUNONONONONO just take your upvote and leave D:

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My only gripe with this is the way the crosslink was done. I feel like it could be grounded better in-universe. You could have a researcher's note at that point, indicating that Beck held up a hand-sign known to be used by fifthist members, or something to that effect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: He'll be in your dreams (dreams) dre-eams, d-d-d-dreams.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wasps, maggots, and clusters of holes all freak me right the hell out. I'm gonna have some trouble listening to Beck for a while.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No, that is most certainly not better. It's a good thing this'll filter out of my memory by bedtime, or it would feature heavily in nightmares.

Aiden, nicely done, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Have you all seen my photo of the dead ichneumon wasp I found with the four-inch long ovipositor? It looks like a long metallic needle.

No, it's not for stinging people. It's for injecting eggs into host larvae through solid wood. Much better.

ETA: I've determined that what I found was a Megarhyssa macrurus.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Body horror and the Fifthists - two great tastes that taste great together.
My first reaction to the product name was 'why would anyone want to clean wasps?' Then we find out what the cleanser actually does. Oh my.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is so fucking weird and gross.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
God damn you, Eldritch. Take your upvote, you monster. Gah.

I'm going to be scratching my skin for the rest of the day.

item.title: Anglo-Saxon Protestants Not Included
item.content:encoded:
I was contemplating a crosslink to psul's Boxes. Remind me to ping them about it later.

+ SPOILARS
– hide block
  • They're always sent from a Foundation front company.
  • They're typically sent to a place with lots of prison inmates.
  • It's a "Stanford-Caldwell Pty." product.
    • Yes, the Caldwell name is a somewhat non-existent reference to Jekeled's skips/tales.
  • The broadcast hours are a reference to Adult Swim's infomercials, and the cover story is an implicit nod to that series.
  • There's a reason why Durham is wearing a lab coat.
  • Same for the woman in the orange jumpsuit.
  • The shortage, in case you hadn't worked it out, is a shortage of D-Class.
  • What horrific wasp plague could be so bad as to warrant the Foundation a) breaking the masquerade, b) selling out in the process c) working with the Fifthists of all people?
    • You may notice that in 1425, the Fifthist cult is impossibly virulent. If they needed to get a cure out fast, it would probably help if the entire consumer culture could be hijacked by a celebrity testimonial.
